title: primula matthioli subsp. sibirica
intro: Primula matthioli subsp. sibirica, also known as Sibirian primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant belonging to the family Primulaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of gastritis and peptic ulcers. The plant is rich in flavonoids and saponins, which contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Sibirian primrose are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
